name: Phil Moore
abstract: Phil Moore (February 20, 1918 – May 13, 1987) was an American jazz pianist, arranger, and bandleader.

about_quotes.speaker: Gerald Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: Phil was my mentor because I met him when I was with Jimmie Lunceford.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gerald Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John Levy
about_quotes.evidence: I did some things with – I don’t know whether the Cabin Boys came in there. No, this is another period. This is a different period. This is much later. But Phil is one of the people that I worked with.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Levy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Cotton Club, '75, co-arranger with Phil Moore. ... And you and Phil Moore did the arranging. Phil Moore and Pete Meyers and Gil Laskey.

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Mingus
about_quotes.evidence: I remember when I was studying with Phil Moore one time... I studied with him, composition. No, I studied with him arranging.

about_quotes.speaker: Dianne Reeves
about_quotes.evidence: And at the time I was working with the great vocal coach Phil Moore.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dianne Reeves interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, October 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Hinton Milt
about_quotes.evidence: I ran into Phil Moore, wonderful pianist, arranger, a dear friend
about_quotes.doc_title: Hinton Milt — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
